channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: TriZetto confirms 3.4M people’s health and personal data was stolen during breach
item.description: Health tech giant TriZetto has confirmed that more than 3.4 million people had personal and health information stolen in a 2024 cyberattack, which the company failed to detect for almost a year.

item.title: Free lance enfin sa 5G sur la bande de 2,1 GHz : ce que ça signifie pour les abonnés mobiles
item.description: Quelques semaines après avoir reçu l'autorisation de déployer des sites 5G sur la bande de fréquences de 2,1 GHz, Free a activé ces antennes et propose pour la première fois la 5G sur trois bandes de fréquences différentes. Une étape importante dans l'évolution de son réseau mobile.

item.title: Le Patron d’Epic Games n’a plus le droit de critiquer le Google Play Store jusqu’en 2032
item.description: Tim Sweeney, le boss d'Epic Games, a signé un accord avec Google l'obligeant, entre autres, à ne plus critiquer le Play Store. Et même d'en dire du bien.

item.title: Indonesia outlines plan to limit under-16s’ access to social media
item.description: Indonesian has outlined new regulations that would restrict users under 16 from accessing digital platforms.

item.title: Fed up with intrusive ads? NymVPN’s latest updates bring ad-blocker tool to desktop apps
item.description: NymVPN’s v2026.4 is now live and brings a new ad-blocker tool to desktop apps, alongside improvements to app accessibility. Here's all you need to know.

item.title: The new iPad Air M4 doesn’t launch until March 11 — but Walmart already has a rare $40 discount
item.description: The all-new iPad Air M4 is available for preorder and ships March 11. Walmart is already offering an incredible $40 discount, bringing the price down to $559.99.

item.title: Crimson Desert will seemingly hit 4K resolutions at higher frame rates, with raytracing on PS5 Pro, thanks to the latest PSSR update
item.description: Pearl Abyss has confirmed that its upcoming open-world action game Crimson Desert will use Sony's updated PSSR (PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution) AI upscaling tech on PS5 Pro.

item.title: Google reveals huge number of zero-days patched in 2025, says worse may be to come as 'AI changes the game'
item.description: The zero-day trend is clear, but things are changing fast, Google security experts warn.

item.title: Doctor Doom Could Be Coming for the Multiverse in ‘Avengers: Doomsday’
item.description: Plus, Mando rides into action in a new look at 'The Mandalorian and Grogu'.

item.title: 11 Best USB Flash Drives (2026): Pen Drives, Thumb Drives, Memory Sticks
item.description: These WIRED-tested memory sticks are a virtual filing cabinet in your pocket.

item.title: Indie games are turning the act of looking into an art
item.description: Adriaan de Jongh and Sylvain Tegroeg did not necessarily set out to create a new genre. But, in some ways, that's exactly what the duo did when they released Hidden Folks in 2017. Hidden object games have been around for decades - they've existed well before computers, when people searched for objects in paintings and […]

item.title: City Detect, which uses AI to help cities stay safe and clean, raises $13M Series A
item.description: City Detect, a company that helps local governments prevent urban decay, is in at least 17 cities so far, including Dallas and Miami.

item.title: I tried Logitech’s new G325 Lightspeed gaming headset, and it was so comfortable I forgot I was wearing it
item.description: The Logitech G325 Lightspeed is a highly comfortable and stylish option that’s perfect for more casual gamers.

item.title: Les États-Unis veulent contrôler l’accès mondial aux puces Nvidia et AMD
item.description: La Maison Blanche entend bien imposer au monde l'IA américaine. Mais un projet de loi pourrait bien mettre un sérieux frein à cette ambition : le gouvernement de Donald Trump voudrait autoriser la vente des GPU Nvidia et AMD aux entreprises étrangères, quel quel soit le pays.

item.title: Quantum scale-up Pasqal plans $2B SPAC listing, promises to ‘remain French’
item.description: A SPAC deal that is set to take French quantum company Pasqal public on the Nasdaq values the company at $2 billion pre-money.

item.title: India’s Karnataka signals intent to ban social media for under-16s
item.description: Karnataka's proposal highlights a growing global debate over children’s rights and online policy.

item.title: La PS5 Slim Digital Edition revient avec une belle promotion chez ce revendeur français
item.description: La PlayStation 5 Slim Digital est une version allégée et dépourvue de lecteur de disque de la célèbre console de Sony, à retrouver pour 429,97 euros au lieu de 499,99 euros chez Electro Dépôt.

item.title: Alerte rouge sur l’iPhone : Apple révèle qu’une opération d’espionnage frappe la France
item.description: Apple a lancé sa première grande alerte de l’année en France. Certains propriétaires d’iPhone ont reçu un message prévenant qu’ils étaient la cible de logiciels espions extrêmement sophistiqués.

item.title: This phone starts fires on purpose
item.description: Until now, most mobile phone companies have worked to ensure their phones won't start fires. (Occasional Samsung devices excepted, of course.) But this week at Mobile World Congress, we found a company that dared to go in a different direction. Oukitel's WP63 rugged smartphone includes a built-in fire starter, and this is what it looks […]

item.title: IKEA's smart donut lamp is finally available to buy — but I'm even more obsessed with these quirky alternatives
item.description: I went down an IKEA lighting rabbit hole, and things got strange.

item.title: Engadget Podcast: Is the MacBook Neo the one?
item.description: It's been a wild week for Apple. After announcing a slew of new hardware, the company capped things off with its cheapest laptop ever: the $599 MacBook Neo. It's low on specs, but high on character and value. In this episode, Devindra and Engadget Deputy Editor Nathan Ingraham dive into the MacBook Neo, as well as the refreshed MacBook Air M5, MacBook Pro M5 Pro/Max, iPad Air M4 and iPhone 17e. Also, Devindra chats with Spencer Ackerman, author of Forever Wars and recent Iron Man comics, about the ongoing battle between Anthropic and the Department of Defense. It turns out the DOD still used C...

item.title: BMW réplique à Tesla : le premier robot humanoïde débarque dans une usine européenne
item.description: BMW vient d'annoncer le déploiement pour la première fois en Europe d'un robot humanoïde dans une ligne de production automobile. À l'usine de Leipzig, un certain AEON s'apprête à cohabiter avec les ouvriers. Promesse technologique ou gadget médiatique ?

item.title: Traveling to South Korea? Jetpac eSIM offers an easy way to stay connected with a 15% exclusive discount
item.description: Install once, land, and connect without dealing with physical SIM cards.

item.title: After Europe, WhatsApp will let rival AI companies offer chatbots in Brazil
item.description: Meta is now allowing rival AI companies to provide their chatbots on WhatsApp to Brazilian users for a fee, a day after the company confirmed a similar decision for users in Europe.

item.title: Test du Mova N1 : le robot laveur de vitres compact et abordable
item.description: Mova, la marque montante de l'écosystème Dreame) tente de nous réconcilier avec la corvée du lavage de vitres, grâce à son N1, un robot lave-vitre qui mise sur la compacité. Pas de valise encombrante ni de station complexe ici : on est sur un format « Mini » qui veut aller à l'essentiel. Mais cette légèreté ne cache-t-elle pas quelques compromis ? Verdict. ...

item.title: 'AI is far from reaching its theoretical capability': Anthropic launches new tool to warn us when jobs might lost to AI
item.description: Anthropic reveals how it is monitoring AI's impacts on the workforce, and how policymakers can protect future workers.

item.title: Tech and finance layoffs: Oracle, Block, Morgan Stanley, Capital One headline brutal week for job losses
item.description: Banking, fintech, and software firms are shedding thousands of jobs, according to recent media reports. It’s not all because of AI. The past week has been a brutal one for many working in the tech and financial industries.

item.title: People Are Convinced Nathan Fillion Is Reviving ‘Firefly’ Via… Instagram Posts?
item.description: Co-stars Gina Torres, Morena Baccarin, Sean Maher, Jewel Staite, and Summer Glau have all played a part in the mysterious stunt.

item.title: Corsair Sabre V2 Carbon Fiber and Magnesium Gaming Mice Review
item.description: It is worth using motorsports materials in a gaming mouse? As long as it's the same price as plastic.

item.title: Marathon is in a sprint
item.description: Marathon is weird as hell - and I mean that in the best possible way. The latest game from Destiny studio Bungie mixes the slick gunplay the developer is known for with a dark, and frankly bizarre, sci-fi universe. It's part of the burgeoning "extraction shooter" subgenre, where teams of players are dropped into a […]

item.title: Dell XPS 14 (2026) review: A beautiful laptop that excels at almost everything… except typing
item.description: There is so much to love about Dell's new XPS 14, it's hard for me to discuss it without sounding like a total fanboy. Its new design makes it incredibly thin and light. And Intel's Panther Lake chips give it a ton of power, even when it comes to games. After reviving the XPS brand from last year's ignominious rebranding, it's as if Dell was laser-focused on fixing all the issues we've had with the XPS 14 so far. Unfortunately, a shallow, unresponsive and hilariously error-prone keyboard kept me from truly loving the XPS 14. Simply put, it has trouble recognizing keys if you press them too qui...

item.title: 'We’d love to make lenses like these' — Fujifilm wants you to vote on the three 'dream lenses' it could make in the future. Here's how to have your say
item.description: Fujifilm hosted a live 'Focus on Glass' event on YouTube, proposing 14 lens concepts for the public to vote on. Which would you like to see become a reality?

item.title: How conspiracy theories spread before the internet, according to Tracy Letts’s ‘Bug’ on Broadway
item.description: Fast Company recently spoke with Namir Smallwood, who is starring opposite Carrie Coon in a 30-year-old play that feels creepily perfect for our era. Conspiracy theories are literally contagious. Recent research on misinformation and how it goes viral across social networks has revealed remarkable parallels to how diseases spread through populations. ...
